issue_body:

Community documentations contains "Report an issue" link within each section that points to a Jira issue tracker, which is no longer used.

Link should point to GHI.

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

for example https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: locale attribute not set after registration
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When a user creates a new account using the direct registration link of our application the user's locale is not saved as an attribute. If the user explicitly switches the current language by the language dropdown, the attribute is set, even if they just switch to english and back to german.

See also [this SO question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/66208538/keycloak-locale-not-set-after-registration) describing the very same behaviour.

The registration link in our app is like this

https://<server>/auth/realms/<realm>/protocol/openid-connect/registrations?client_id=<id>&response_type=code&ui_locales=de&kc_locale=de&redirect_uri=<callback_url>

as mentioned [here ](https://stackoverflow.com/a/53264417/2217962) and in the referred keycloak mailing list archive.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

Every user should have a "locale" attribute after registration, containing the language used during registration.

### Actual behavior

"locale" attribute is not set at all.

### How to Reproduce?

* Start keycloak
* Use registration link from above
* Register a new user
* Go to user detail page - locale dropdown is empty
* Read user attrbutes - locale is not set

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Propagate locale when using app initiated registration URL

Fixes #13505

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Exceptions are not visible in testsuite output
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm running the test suite under maven with `-Pauth-server-quarkus`. 
Exceptions in the server are not visible in the log output.

The server logs are also missing from the testsuite output.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

When code run by the server throws an exception, lets say `NullPointerException`, I'd like full backtrace to be displayed in the test suite output.

### Actual behavior

The exception from failing test case is shown, but the original exception in the server that caused the test case to fail is not shown.

### How to Reproduce?

Modify server to throw unhandled exception.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message: Forward quarkus server output to console in testsuite

issue_title: Misleading/unhelpful error for invalid SAML responses
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When an invalid SAML response is received during login, the error displayed is the unhelpful:

> We are sorry...
> invalidFederatedIdentityActionMessage

There are 2 issues with this:

1. The message key ("invalidFederatedIdentityActionMessage") is displayed instead of the message from properties file ("Invalid or missing action")
2. The message (key or text) is an inaccurate description of the problem

### Version

8.0.2, 15.1.1

### Expected behavior

Error message displayed when receiving invalid SAML response:
> We are sorry...
> Invalid response from identity provider.

Error message displayed when receiving SAML response with invalid signature:
> We are sorry...
> Invalid signature in response from identity provider.


### Actual behavior

Error message displayed when receiving invalid SAML response or response with invalid signature:
> We are sorry...
> invalidFederatedIdentityActionMessage


### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure Keycloak with a SAML Identity Provider with an invalid or an expired Validating X509 Certificate and Validate Signature turned ON.
2. Attempt to log in using that Identity Provider.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Improve error messages for invalid SAML responses

Closes #13534

issue_title: Reuse of token in TOTP is possible
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

According to the document, the verifier should not accept the second attempt of the OTP after successful validation but KeyCloak is allowing using of the same OTP token multiple times within the timeframe.  

https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6238.html#section-5

Note that a prover may send the same OTP inside a given time-step window
multiple times to a verifier. The verifier MUST NOT accept the second
attempt of the OTP after the successful validation has been issued for the
first OTP, which ensures one-time only use of an OTP.

### Version

18.0.0

issue_title: Offline User Session dead lock caused by PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

There is a deadlock under some circumstances with latest Keycloak.

In case there are 2 cluster nodes and both updating DB at the same time from persister:

Node1 calling something like (real Ids replaced with placeholders):
```
update OFFLINE_USER_SESSION set LAST_SESSION_REFRESH=1656332405 where REALM_ID='X' and 
OFFLINE_FLAG='1' and (USER_SESSION_ID in ('a' , 'b' , 'c' , 'd'))
```
Node2 calling something like (real Ids replaced with placeholders):
```
update OFFLINE_USER_SESSION set LAST_SESSION_REFRESH=1656332405 where REALM_ID='Y' and 
OFFLINE_FLAG='1' and (USER_SESSION_ID in ('aa' , 'bb' , 'cc' , 'dd'))
```
From the lock monitor, both are named JPQL "updateUserSessionLastSessionRefresh" called from different RH-SSO node.

JPQL "updateUserSessionLastSessionRefresh" definition:
```
update PersistentUserSessionEntity sess set lastSessionRefresh = :lastSessionRefresh where sess.realmId = :realmId AND 
sess.offline = :offline AND sess.userSessionId IN (:userSessionIds)
```
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19.0.1/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/session/PersistentUserSessionEntity.java#L38-L39

When token refresh or token introspection is requested to an offline session, RH-SSO need to update LAST_SESSION_REFRESH culumn of the OFFLINE_USER_SESSION table record,
but instead of issuing the update query immediately, RH-SSO enqueue the update request in AbstractLastSessionRefreshStore, and update them periodically.
JPQL "updateUserSessionLastSessionRefresh" is the query for this bulk update.

Since token refresh or token introspection requests from clients are distributed to RH-SSO nodes,
it may happen that multiple LAST_SESSION_REFRESH update queries are issued from different RH-SSO nodes at the same time.
If it happens to multiple sessions, it ends up in the dead lock because RH-SSO node wraps the multiple update request into one transaction.

### Dead lock flow example

If clients requests offline sessions X and Y to different RH-SSO nodes(A and B), RH-SSO Node A and B issues update queries in one transaction. This will cause dead lock. 

```
RH-SSO Node A ----> Begin Tx ----> Update Realm001 session X ---------------> Update Realm002 session Y  -----------> Dead 
Lock!(Waiting for Realm002 session Y)
RH-SSO Node B --------> Begin Tx ------------> Update Realm002 session Y -------------> Update Realm001 session X  -> Dead 
Lock!(Waiting for Realm001 session X)
```

### Solution proposal

Sorting the session update order will resolve the dead lock.

I think sorting the items by key here will work.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19.0.1/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/sessions/PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore.java#L64
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19.0.1/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/sessions/PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore.java#L72

```
RH-SSO Node A ----> Begin Tx ----> Update Realm001 session X ---------------> Update Realm002 session Y ---> Commit ---> 
Finish!
RH-SSO Node B --------> Begin Tx ------------> Update Realm001 session X ------> (Wait for Realm001 session X lock) -----> 
Update Realm002 session Y --> Commit --> Finish!
``` 

Could you check my analysis? and if it valid, could you provide a fix and include it to the next RH-SSO 7.6 patch update?


### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

This is related to ticket 293.

Originally reported by @ynojima
commit_message:
Use separate transactions for each bulk update of offline sessions in PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore to avoid deadlocks
closes #13684

issue_title: Switching Locale after Completing an admin triggered required action yields an error
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

One way to produce this is to

- install Keycloak
- login to admin console and create new realm "test" . Perform operations below with the new realm
- configure mail server
- configure more than one locale for the current theme in the "test" realm
- create user with email address
- as an admin go to users -> the new user -> credentials
- Trigger a credential reset with the reset action VERIFY_EMAIL
- Wait for the email to arrive
- Open the link from the email
- click on the button "Click here to proceed" to complete the required action
- now switch to another locale
- there you are with an error page for "Cookie not found. Please make sure cookies are enabled in your browser"

In the log, there is this:
```
2022-08-22 18:04:20,178 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-61) type=CUSTOM_REQUIRED_ACTION_ERROR, realmId=41bda29c-2586-45f1-bc08-f072929fddf7, clientId=null, userId=null, ipAddress=127.0.0.1, error=cookie_not_found
```



### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

2 possibilities how to fix it:

1) Locale is switched. Ideally it keeps to be switched for the current user, who triggered the action (if possible). Or it is kept as a KC_LOCALE in the browser

2) The alternative solution is, that on the "Info" page (the page displayed after user clicked on the `Click here to proceed`), there won't be a choice to switch the locale.

The solution (1) is better, but not sure it is possible. As I think that after click `Click here to proceed`, there is not anymore any authenticationSession available and hence this will require changing locale without authenticationSession available, which might be very tricky (or impossible) to do.

issue_title: `--optimized` is not supported by `kc.sh export`
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I have a realm export script that essentially runs:
```
# note: kc.sh export briefly starts the server including binding a http port, which fails because 8080 is already in use. So we give it a random port instead.
KC_HTTP_PORT=0 kc.sh export --file  ${_EXPORT_FILE_TMP} --users same_file
```

With KC 19.0.1 this logs:
> Changes detected in configuration. Updating the server image.

and then goes on to rebuild, which I don't want and which wasn't there with KC 18.0.2.
I also don't see how the runtime config property `KC_HTTP_PORT` is relevant for rebuilding?

Adding `--optimized` doesn't work:
> Unknown option: '--optimized'

### Version

19.0.1

issue_title: 19 - update-email feature - email change does not affect the username when "Email as username" option is checked
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hello,
I am testing the new update_email feature.
If I have the option "Email as username" active when changing email, it changes the email but not its username.
Is there any other option so that the email change affects the username when the "Email as username" option is checked ?
Thanks.

### Version

19

### Expected behavior

If the login option "Email as username" is active, when I change the email it must also change the username.

### Actual behavior

If the login option "Email as username" is active, when I change the email I have the old email as username.

### How to Reproduce?

1. activate the update-email feature
2. have the login option "email as username" activated
3. then log in with a user 
4. go to "personnal info" 
5. and modify his email 
6. the email has been changed but not the username 

### Anything else?

The new feature [update_email](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/7943)
commit_message:
update-email: email change does not affect the username when "Email as username" option is checked (#15583)

Closes #13988

issue_title: Requests to health endpoint still dispatched to worker threads when `http-relative-path` is set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

This issue was supposed to be fixed by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/11507:

>Avoid executing health and metrics endpoint in a worker thread as they run in their own worker thread, and avoid creating a KeycloakSession every time those endpoints are invoked. The current behavior is causing transactions to not disassociate from the calling thread and causing Arjuna to wait for them to timeout. Eventually, the server might block during the shutdown.

However, the previous fix did not take into account when the `http-relative-path` is set so that health/metrics requests are not ignored by the `QuarkusRequestFilter` and  are still dispatched to worker threads.

### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

Do not dispatch health/metrics requests to a worker thread.

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Run the server as follows:

```
./kc.sh -Dquarkus.transaction-manager.default-transaction-timeout=1s start-dev --health-enabled true --http-relative-path /auth/
```

Then, execute multiple requests to `/auth/health` until you get log entries similar to:

```
2022-08-25 19:42:41,449 WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.arjuna] (executor-thread-1) ARJUNA012077: Abort called on already aborted atomic action 0:ffffc0a8000b:8ca1:6307f782:213                        
2022-08-25 19:42:42,449 WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.arjuna] (Transaction Reaper) ARJUNA012117: TransactionReaper::check processing TX 0:ffffc0a8000b:8ca1:6307f782:291 in state  RUN                
2022-08-25 19:42:42,449 WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.arjuna] (Transaction Reaper Worker 0) ARJUNA012095: Abort of action id 0:ffffc0a8000b:8ca1:6307f782:291 invoked while multiple threads active within it.               
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Related to the reported bug in [JIRA](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-16009), UserInfo endpoint should check duplicated parameters of requests but does not check.


issue_title: Setting responseMode in AuthorizationRequest.Metadata does not take effect
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

**Problem** :  With org.keycloak:keycloak-authz-client it is not possible to set "response_mode" in  AuthorizationRequest

response_mode is not sent to the server, when AuthorizationRequest.Metadata#[setResponseMode](https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/18.0/javadocs/org/keycloak/representations/idm/authorization/AuthorizationRequest.Metadata.html) is set to "decision" 
​
```
       AuthorizationRequest request = new AuthorizationRequest();
        request.addPermission(resource, scope);
        request.setMetadata(metadata);
        request.getMetadata().setResponseMode("decision");
```

The response_mode "decision" is required to receive overall decision from the server.

 In  org.keycloak.authorization.client.util.[HttpMethodAuthenticator](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bfce612641a70e106b20b136431f0e4046b5c37f/authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/HttpMethodAuthenticator.java#L118-L129) the setResponseMode is not taken into account



### Version

18.0

### Expected behavior

AuthorizationRequest  sent to the server has the option to set response_mode

### Actual behavior

AuthorizationRequest  sent to the server does not has the option to set response_mode

### How to Reproduce?

- Send AuthorizationRequest with setResponseMode to decision
 
```
AuthorizationRequest` request = new AuthorizationRequest();
 request.addPermission(resource, scope);
 request.setMetadata(metadata);
 request.getMetadata().setResponseMode("decision");
```

- The response has the access_token instead of overall decision

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: DefaultBruteForceProtector leverages a single thread to write success/failed events
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/DefaultBruteForceProtector.java uses a single thread/queue to update the database for success/failed login attempts.

The idea behind it is to prevent multiple concurrent writes to overwrite each-other. The problem with that though is most installation of Keycloak is leveraging a cluster with multiple nodes, which means this doesn't really do anything other than introduce a bottleneck. 

This is a follow-up to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/7748, where it was observed that during both success and failed login attempts a wait of up to 5 seconds is introduced waiting for the queue to process the event. It can be questioned if this is a good idea or not for failed login attempts, but it certainly isn't a good idea to do this for successful logins. 

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Following this [discussion](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/10937), we are experience the problem. 

Keycloak has single session and with for example 3 tabs opened, using sessionStorage as proposed by [angular-oauth2-oidc](https://github.com/manfredsteyer/angular-oauth2-oidc), when we have token rotation enabled it doesn't work, because we can only use the lastest RT.

### Version

18.0.2

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should do a rotation per initial delivered token, so each tab can use their own RT (table instead of single record)

### Actual behavior

Only one of the tabs work as expected (not sure if first opened or the last one)

### How to Reproduce?

create spa that uses sessionStorage to keep the RT and open multiple tabs

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Improvements to refresh token rotation with multiple tabs (#29966)

Closes #14122

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: Validate auth time when max_age is sent to brokered OPs
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

After https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8183, it should be possible to propagate the `max_age` param to brokered OPs.

However, the `auth_time` claim from the ID Token is not validated to make sure that re-authentication happened.

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

Fail broker authentication if `max_age` is set but the brokered OP does not re-authenticate the user.

### Actual behavior

The broker authentication is successful even though re-authentication did not happen as per `max_age` parameter.

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

_No response_

issue_title: IDP Provider is hidden from the login form after the back button is pressed
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When there's an IDP in the KeyCloak realm (eg.: Google or Facebook) AND in the _First Broker Login_ authentication flow the _Review Profile_ is set then I can hide the given IDP from the login page.



### Version

19.0.1-legacy

### Expected behavior

After the following steps below the IPD should not disappear from the login page.

### Actual behavior

After the following steps below the IPD is filtered out by [LoginFormsUtil::63](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/LoginFormsUtil.java#L63-L67). 

### How to Reproduce?

Steps required:
 - open a keycloak login page (for example we have one here: fiok.bkk.hu)
 - click on the _New user? Register_ button to open the registration screen
 - do not fill the registration form, but click on the _Already have an account? Log in!_ button to go back to the login screen
 - click on any IDP you like (eg.: click on the Google login option)
 - on the _Please provide your email address_ do not provide your e-mail address but click the back button in your browser. Go back to the login screen
 - the IDP disappeared from the login screen

### Anything else?

I think the LoginFormsUtil should not filter out based on the `existingIdp` if there's no user in the context.
commit_message:
Should not hide IDP from login page (#14174)

Closes #14173
